article_title: Python 3.15 Hits Feature Freeze as Release Manager Postpones Beta 1 to May 7, Locking in Lazy Imports, frozendict, and a New Profiler
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Python 3.15 Hits Feature Freeze as Release Manager Postpones Beta 1 to May 7, Locking in Lazy Imports, frozendict, and a New Profiler
content_preview.summary: Release manager Hugo van Kemenade postponed Python 3.15.0 beta 1 by two days to May 7, 2026, marking the feature freeze for one of the language's largest releases in years.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Python 3.15 reaches its feature freeze this week. Release manager Hugo van Kemenade [announced on the python.org committers forum](https://discuss.python.org/t/python-3-15-0-beta-1-is-near/107193) that he would slip the first beta of Python 3.15 by two days, writing: "I'm going to postpone for a couple of days, until Thursday 2026-05-07 to give a bit more time to get the last few things in." The PEP 790 release schedule has been updated accordingly: [3.15.0 beta 1 is now dated](http...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong technical News piece, 889 words. Clear progression from postponement → feature slate → JIT/perf → Steering Council schedule pressure → surrounding May news. Internal cross-reference to alpha 7 article verified to exist on disk.

source_verification.source-0_discuss_python: Verified verbatim: Hugo van Kemenade postponement quote 'I'm going to postpone for a couple of days, until Thursday 2026-05-07 to give a bit more time to get the last few things in.'
source_verification.source-1_pep790: Verified: '3.15.0 beta 1: Thursday, 2026-05-07 (No new features beyond this point.)'; release manager Hugo van Kemenade.
source_verification.source-2_docs_python: Verified verbatim: PEP 810 explicit lazy imports, PEP 814 frozendict, PEP 799 dedicated profiling package (Tachyon), PEP 661 sentinel; -X lazy_imports CLI option, PYTHON_LAZY_IMPORTS env var, sys.set_lazy_imports/get_lazy_imports.
source_verification.source-3_python_release: Verified: '6-7% geometric mean performance improvement on x86-64 Linux over the standard interpreter, and 12-13% speedup on AArch64 macOS over the tail-calling interpreter'; Windows 64-bit binaries switched to tail-calling interpreter; PEP slate listed.
source_verification.source-4_infoworld: Verified: 'Get started with Python's new frozendict type', 'frozendict... fills a long-desired niche in Python — and can be used in more places than ordinary dictionaries'.
source_verification.source-5_realpython: Verified: PEP 772 accepted April 16 by PSF and Steering Council; 'five-member Packaging Council that will make binding decisions about packaging standards and tools'; beta 1 marks feature freeze; 'no new PEPs land in 3.15'.
source_verification.source-6_three_weeks: Verified: Barry Warsaw 'We're now three weeks and a day until 3.15 beta 1 and feature freeze' (April 13 2026); 'the longer you wait to submit to the SC, the less of a chance your PEP will make it in'; Hugo van Kemenade reply 'the three weeks and a day includes SC pronouncement and getting it implemented and reviewed and merged into main'.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim. PEP numbers (810, 814, 799, 798, 686, 728, 747, 782, 803, 661, 772), JIT speedups (6-7% / 12-13%), and dates all verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. Conservative qualifications about PEP 790 'modified or deleted' clause and benchmark drift between beta 1 and GA.
editor_notes.originality: Builds on prior Machine Herald alpha 7 coverage with proper internal cross-reference. Distinct news angle (feature freeze → beta 1 postponement) makes this a natural follow-up.
concerns: discuss.python.org (the python.org committers/steering-council forum) is not on the project allowlist. It is the official discussion venue for Python core development; it carries primary-source weight. Recommend adding.
recommendations: Document the allowlist gap in a corrections file (advisory).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Cryptography, sources, and content all clean. Article is editorially distinct from prior alpha 7 coverage and properly cross-references it.
